242:                     connectionFailureCount = 0;

244:                     lastConnectTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
245:                     connectFuture = null;

258:             ++connectionFailureCount;

260:             while (e.getCause() != null) {
261:                 e = e.getCause();
262:             }
263:             final String nextRetryMsg = " (Next retry in " + computeNextRetryConnectDelay() + " milliseconds)";
264:             if (e instanceof IOException) {
265:                 fixSession.getLog().onErrorEvent(e.getClass().getName() + " during connection to " + socketAddress + ": " + e + nextRetryMsg);
266:                 fixSession.getStateListener().onConnectException(fixSession.getSessionID(), (IOException) e);
267:             } else {

302:                 millis = reconnectIntervalInMillis[reconnectIntervalInMillis.length - 1];
303:             } else {

Implemented a generic JavaParser rewrite for the Mina 2.2.1 breakage and validated it on `quickfixj-core`.

What changed:
- `new SslFilter(SSLContext, boolean)` -> `new SslFilter(SSLContext)`
- Removed chained calls to deprecated/removed `setUseClientMode(...)`
- Removed `initiateHandshake(...)` calls
- Rewrote `getSslSession(ioSession)` to `((SSLSession) ioSession.getAttribute(SslFilter.SSL_SECURED))`
- Removed the obsolete `PEER_ADDRESS` attribute write
- Widened overridden `onPreAdd(...)` throws clauses to `Exception` when delegating to `super.onPreAdd(...)`

Validation:
- `javaparser-base-template` compiles
- Applied to `quickfixj-core/src/main/java` and `src/test/java`
- `mvn -pl quickfixj-core -am test-compile` passes

The rule is source-root driven and contains no QuickFIX/J-specific identifiers beyond the dependency API names.
